title: Consiliency projections index (projections.index.v1)
description: A deterministic pure-merge index of per-artifact projection manifests (+ portal_projection.v2 envelopes + refresh sidecars). Carries NO generated_at / generated_at_commit so a --check rebuild is byte-identical across source commits that touch no manifest (determinism). Per-entry pinned_commit carries the meaningful provenance. The wire schema id is 'projections.index.v1' (non-namespaced, per DESIGN §2.3 — the interchangeability wire value the producer emits) while the file and loader key follow the package's hyphen/snake conventions. v0.4.1: facts_path/facts_digest/pinned_commit are conditionally required (proj-code kinds only); proj-S-certified requires source_S_digest instead and is the only kind permitted a certified-tier maturity_label. v0.4.2: the per-kind maturity caps are TWO-SIDED enums — proj-code is [presence-only, hash-checked] (never realized-edge-observed or any certified tier) and proj-S-certified is [realized-edge-observed, certified] (floor-revert semantics), replacing the one-sided not:certified guard. v0.6.0 (CS-1.4): splits the single certified rung into parity-certified (byte-parity cert, no authority event) and authority-certified (parity PLUS a verified, human-ratified authority event) — the bare 'certified' id is kept in both maturity_label enums below ONLY as a deprecated alias of parity-certified (never authority-certified), so existing producers/consumers keep validating; new entries should emit parity-certified or authority-certified. See docs/design/CS-1.4-certified-label-rescope.md and core/registries/maturity-labels.json.

source_S_digest.description: REQUIRED for kind proj-S-certified: the canon semantic-content digest of the desired-state graph S this certified projection renders (the certificate's desired_graph_digest). It is the neutral index field the aggregator maps from spec's graph_digest / desired_graph_digest, following the same naming pattern as code_head_sha -> pinned_commit. A certified proj-S pins S, not a code commit — pinned_commit/facts_* are proj-code concepts and are conditionally required for the proj-code kinds only (v0.4.1).

maturity_label.description: 'certified' is a DEPRECATED alias of parity-certified, kept for existing producers/consumers (CS-1.4). See the per-kind caps below for which entries may use which values.

branch.description: OPTIONAL pipeline-owned contract branch this projection was generated on (DESIGN §11.1). Omitted keeps the entry a pure function of the manifest per §2.3 / §10.4 (single pin per commit; currency is a display-time comparison, not a per-branch generation matrix). Populated only when the branch is available in the generation context; never required for pure-merge determinism.

refresh_status.description: REFRESH-LAYER field (§5), NOT a manifest verdict. Merged from the committed <artifact>.refresh.json SIDECAR, never from the manifest (manifests are additionalProperties:false single-writer gate records). Absent when no sidecar exists.

display_route.description: Present for parity-certified / authority-certified entries (from portal_projection.v2); null / absent otherwise.

maturity_label.description: proj-code kinds are capped at hash-checked (no S-model/parity cert): presence-only | hash-checked only — never realized-edge-observed nor any certified tier (certified, parity-certified, authority-certified).

a proj-S-certified entry either floor-reverts to realized-edge-observed (cert bind failed) or carries a certified-tier label when the C1-C5 cert bind passes — matching the slice-1 gate's floor-revert semantics. Any certified-tier value is permitted ONLY for this kind (two-sided cap: proj-code never reaches it). CS-1.4 splits that tier: parity-certified (byte parity, no authority event) is the honest label for what this kind proves today; authority-certified additionally requires a verified authority event (enforced upstream by the delivery/gate layer, not by this schema). 'certified' remains valid here as a DEPRECATED alias of parity-certified.
